headline: GE Aerospace CEO Is Optimistic for More China Deals
summary: In an interview at the IATA annual meeting in Rio de Janeiro, GE Aerospace CEO Larry Culp remained optimistic that GE could secure more aircraft-engine orders from China following a meeting between President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing the previous month, after which their manufacturing partner, Boeing Co. secured an initial batch of commitments.

headline: Etihad Airways CEO Says Carrier Is Discounting Tickets
summary: Etihad Airways CEO Antonoaldo Neves speaks to Bloomberg's Guy Johnson at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) conference in Rio de Janeiro. He says the carrier is discounting tickets by keeping fares at a stable level despite the 70% jump in fuel costs. Neves also talks about securing delivery dates for new aircraft from Boeing and Airbus SE.

headline: A $100 Billion Fuel-Price Shock Is Pushing Airlines Back Into Crisis Mode
summary: Airlines face a fuel‑price shock and aging fleets that threaten post‑pandemic recovery, forcing route cuts and raising costs as industry profits plunge and margins tighten across 2026.

headline: The Boeing Air India Crash Was Almost a Year Ago. The Full Investigation Report Is Coming Soon.
summary: Boeing  is approaching a solemn anniversary: It has been almost one year since the fatal Air India Flight 171.  On June 12, 2025, a Boeing 787 crashed shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad, India, killing 229 passengers, 12 crew, and 19 people on the ground.  The crash’s anniversary signals the imminent arrival of the full accident investigation report from India’s Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau.

headline: Riyadh Air takes delivery of first Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners
summary: Saudi Arabia's Riyadh Air's first two Boeing 787 Dreamliner jets are delivered as the Kingdom's new national carrier prepares to take off, launching five new destinations. CEO Tony Douglas speaks to CNBC's Dan Murphy on Access Middle East.

headline: Do Riyadh Air 787 Deliveries Shift Boeing’s (BA) Narrative From Operational Strain To Strategic Progress?
summary: Boeing and Riyadh Air announced that the airline’s first two 787 Dreamliners were delivered and arrived in Riyadh in early June 2026, marking a key step toward the carrier’s planned network of more than 100 destinations by 2030. This milestone comes as Boeing works through regulatory scrutiny, certification delays, and production challenges that are drawing as much attention as its new aircraft deliveries. Next, we’ll examine how Boeing’s ongoing certification delays and operational...
